title: OpenBody Controlled-Vocabulary File
description: One open, registry-backed vocabulary (SPEC §5.9): the recommended canonical tokens for a single field (e.g. Block.scoring.scheme, disciplines, setRole). The spec defines the MECHANISM (recommended canon token + namespaced fallback + lossless opaque round-trip); this ships the CONTENT on the registry's own cadence. Unknown tokens MUST still round-trip — this file never closes the set. Data is CC0.

vocabulary.description: Stable kebab-case name of this vocabulary (e.g. `block-scoring-scheme`).

field.description: The model field(s) this vocabulary supplies, for human reference (e.g. `Block.scoring.scheme`).

specRef.description: SPEC section that defines the field (e.g. `§5.4`).

open.description: Always true for these vocabularies — the set is open and unknown tokens round-trip (§5.9). Present for clarity; a closed enum is defined in the spec, not here.

token.description: The on-the-wire value, exactly as it appears in records. Per-field casing follows the spec's own examples: lowercase snake/kebab for most fields (`amrap`, `marked_weight`, `vertical-pull`), but acronym/mixed forms where the spec uses them (`RPE`, `%1RM`, `FTP`, `maxHR`). Namespaced extensions (`acme:foo`) are NOT listed here.

aliases.description: Common synonyms/spellings a producer might emit; a consumer MAY fold these to `token`.

appliesTo.description: Optional narrowing: disciplines/contexts this token is typically used in (display/filtering hint, not a constraint).
